mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns over 60 days in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which supports near-term equity momentum and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of documented supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate forces monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when both proxies rise simultaneously, exceeding 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which elevates corporate earnings trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates economic stagnation that reduces business investment and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market rallies on stagnation signals due to anticipated rate cuts, yielding positive returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized supply-side capacity growth, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between production and employment breaks the signal, resulting in negative median returns over 5 days.
